meta.title: Flink E2E Test — Module 7 (BP Variability) + Module 8 (Comorbidity Interaction)

module7_bp_variability.description: Module 7 receives BPReading JSON on ingestion.vitals, computes 7d/30d variability metrics, morning surge, dipping, crisis detection, and white-coat/masked HTN detection. Emits BPVariabilityMetrics to flink.bp-variability-metrics. Crisis/acute-surge readings are side-output to ingestion.safety-critical.

scenarios.clinical_profile: Uncontrolled hypertensive with morning surge pattern. 7 days of morning (high ~152-155) + evening (low ~116-117) readings.
scenarios.expected_outcome: Surge classification detected, variability metrics computed over 7d window

scenarios.clinical_profile: Well-controlled hypertensive. 5 days of stable readings: morning ~122-125, evening ~118-120.
scenarios.expected_outcome: BP control status = CONTROLLED, low variability, no crisis/surge flags

scenarios.clinical_profile: Patient with a sudden spike to SBP=192/DBP=112 (SBP >= 180 threshold). Heart rate elevated at 105.
scenarios.expected_outcome: crisis_flag=true, reading routed to ingestion.safety-critical via side-output

scenarios.clinical_profile: High clinic readings (~155-158) but normal home readings (~122-124). 5 days of alternating clinic/home pairs.

flink.bp-variability-metrics.description: One BPVariabilityMetrics emitted per input BPReading. Each contains: 7d/30d ARV/SD/CV, variability classification, morning surge, dip ratio, BP control status, white-coat/masked flags, crisis flag, data quality depth.

sample_output_fields.sd_sbp_7d: double (standard deviation of SBP over 7 days)
sample_output_fields.morning_surge_today: double (today morning avg - last night avg)
sample_output_fields.morning_surge_7d_avg: double (7-day rolling average of morning surge)

ingestion.safety-critical (BP).description: Crisis readings (SBP >= 180 or DBP >= 120) are side-output to safety-critical topic for immediate physician notification.

module8_comorbidity_interaction.description: Module 8 receives CanonicalEvent JSON on enriched-patient-events-v1. Maintains per-patient ComorbidityState (active medications, labs, vitals, symptoms). Evaluates 17 CID rules (CID-01..CID-17) on each event. Emits CIDAlert to alerts.comorbidity-interactions. HALT-severity alerts are side-output to ingestion.safety-critical.

scenarios.clinical_profile: Patient on triple nephrotoxic combination: RASi (ramipril) + SGLT2i (empagliflozin) + diuretic (chlorthalidone). Weight dropped >2 kg in 7 days (dehydration precipitant).
scenarios.recommended_action: Immediately hold diuretic, recheck renal function within 48h

scenarios.clinical_profile: Patient on SGLT2i (dapagliflozin) reporting nausea — euglycemic DKA risk even with normal glucose. Module 8 accepts 'NAUSEA' or 'VOMITING' as separate symptom types.
scenarios.recommended_action: Check blood ketones immediately, hold SGLT2i pending results

scenarios.clinical_profile: Patient on thiazide (hydrochlorothiazide) with fasting blood glucose rising from 110 to 130 mg/dL (delta +20, exceeds 15 mg/dL threshold).
scenarios.recommended_action: Review thiazide necessity, consider alternative antihypertensive, recheck FBG in 4 weeks

scenarios.clinical_profile: Patient on SGLT2i (empagliflozin) prescribed NSAID (ibuprofen). NSAIDs reduce SGLT2i efficacy and increase AKI risk.
scenarios.recommended_action: Consider NSAID alternatives, monitor renal function if continued

alerts.comorbidity-interactions.description: CIDAlert objects with alertId, patientId, ruleId, severity, triggerSummary, medicationsInvolved, recommendedAction, suppressionKey, createdAt, correlationId.

alerts.triggerSummary: HALT: Triple whammy AKI risk. Patient on RASi + SGLT2i + diuretic with weight drop >2kg in 7 days
HALT: Euglycemic DKA risk. Patient on SGLT2i with nausea/vomiting symptoms
alerts.note: Fired twice — once on NAUSEA symptom event, once on glucose LAB_RESULT event (both independently trigger CID-04 evaluation)

alerts.triggerSummary: PAUSE: Thiazide-associated glucose rise. FBG increased 20 mg/dL (threshold: 15)
WARNING: NSAIDs + SGLT2i increase AKI risk and reduce SGLT2i efficacy

ingestion.safety-critical (CID HALT).description: HALT-severity CID alerts are side-output to safety-critical for immediate physician notification. Only HALT severity triggers this — PAUSE and SOFT_FLAG do not.

module8.halt_via_safety.detail: 3 HALT alerts routed to safety-critical side-output

flink_orchestrator_fixes.description: Fixes applied to FlinkJobOrchestrator.java for Flink 2.x compatibility during this e2e test session.

fixes.error: IllegalStateException: Found duplicate transactionalIdPrefix for multiple Kafka sinks: null
fixes.fix: Each KafkaSink gets unique transactionalIdPrefix + DeliveryGuarantee.AT_LEAST_ONCE

fixes.error: Assigned key must not be null (from .keyBy(BPReading::getPatientId))
fixes.fix: Null-safe BPReadingDeserializer with try-catch + .filter(r -> r != null && r.getPatientId() != null) before keyBy

fixes.error: OffsetsInitializer.latest() skipped events produced before job submission

cid_rule_reference.description: All 17 CID rules implemented in Module 8, aligned with DD#7 spec and evaluator source code. Rules tested in this e2e run marked with *.

HALT_rules.name: Triple Whammy AKI
HALT_rules.trigger: ACEi/ARB + SGLT2i + diuretic + precipitant (weight drop >2kg/7d OR eGFR decline >15%/14d OR illness)

HALT_rules.name: Hyperkalemia Cascade
HALT_rules.trigger: ACEi/ARB + finerenone + K+ >5.3 AND rising trajectory

HALT_rules.name: Hypoglycemia Masking
HALT_rules.trigger: Insulin/SU + beta-blocker + glucose <60 + NO symptom report (masked)

HALT_rules.name: Severe Hypotension Risk
HALT_rules.trigger: >=3 antihypertensives + SGLT2i + SBP <95 (or SBP drop >30 from 7d avg)

PAUSE_rules.name: Thiazide Glucose Rise
PAUSE_rules.trigger: Thiazide active + FBG delta >15 mg/dL in 14 days

PAUSE_rules.name: ACEi/ARB Renal Dip
PAUSE_rules.trigger: ACEi/ARB + eGFR drop >25% + >6 weeks since initiation

PAUSE_rules.name: Statin Myalgia
PAUSE_rules.trigger: Statin active + new muscle pain/myalgia symptoms

PAUSE_rules.name: GLP-1RA GI Intolerance
PAUSE_rules.trigger: GLP-1RA + persistent GI symptoms (>=48h) + weight drop >1.5 kg/7d

PAUSE_rules.name: Dual Deterioration
PAUSE_rules.trigger: Concurrent glucose AND BP worsening (>10 mg/dL, >10 mmHg) without medication change in past 14d

SOFT_FLAG_rules.name: Genital Infection + SGLT2i
SOFT_FLAG_rules.trigger: History of genital infection + SGLT2i active

SOFT_FLAG_rules.name: SGLT2i + NSAID AKI Risk
SOFT_FLAG_rules.trigger: SGLT2i + NSAID co-prescribed (increases AKI risk, reduces SGLT2i efficacy)

SOFT_FLAG_rules.name: Salt Sensitivity Signal
SOFT_FLAG_rules.trigger: Salt-sensitive patient profile + sodium-retaining drug active
